// Solution to Exercise 1
//
//Notes about this solution:
// - it does not handle all the possible errors generated by fork() and wait() gracefully
//   we did not stody them, so a similar solution is considered to be correct for our puroises
// - it is preferred for child process to exit with _exit() instead of exit(). In this example
//   their use is equivalent, but there might be corner cases in which _exit() is safer. 
  
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <sys/wait.h>
#include <errno.h>

int main(int argc, char *argv[]) {

    if (argc != 2) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Usage: %s <n>\n", argv[0]);
        return 1;
    }

    int n = atoi(argv[1]);   // conversion using atoi()

    if (n <= 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: n must be a positive integer.\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Create n child processes
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

        pid_t pid = fork();

        if (pid < 0) {
            perror("fork");
            return 1;
        }

        if (pid == 0) {
            // Child process
            srand((unsigned int)getpid());
            int r = rand() % 256;   // 0..255

            printf("Child PID=%ld generated %d\n",
                   (long)getpid(), r);

            _exit(r);
        }
        // Parent continues loop
    }

    // Parent collects results
    int max_val = -1;
    pid_t max_pid = -1;

    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {

        int status;
        pid_t child_pid = wait(&status);

        if (WIFEXITED(status)) {
            int value = WEXITSTATUS(status);

            if (value > max_val) {
                max_val = value;
                max_pid = child_pid;
            }
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr,
                    "Child PID=%ld terminated abnormally.\n",
                    (long)child_pid);
        }
    }

    printf("Highest number: %d (generated by PID=%ld)\n",
           max_val, (long)max_pid);

    return 0;
}
